zh.lifeSummary: 當General Richard Marion Hughes於1834年6月19日出生在美國田納西州漢密爾頓時，他的父親William Hughes29歲，母親Matilda Clingman29歲。他於1851年9月15日與Mary Elizabeth Magby在美國阿肯色州克拉克結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他於1900年住在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林亨德森 堂史普且於1910年住在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林俾斯麥。他去世於1914年3月在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当General Richard Marion Hughes于1834年6月19日出生在美国田纳西州漢密爾頓时，他的父亲William Hughes29岁，母亲Matilda Clingman29岁。他于1851年9月15日与Mary Elizabeth Magby在美国阿肯色州克拉克结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他于1900年住在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林亨德森 堂史普且于1910年住在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林俾斯麥。他去世于1914年3月在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hughes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Hugh with genitival or excrescent -s.
Irish and Scottish: adopted as an equivalent of Gaelic surnames based on the personal name Aodh ‘fire’, for example Ó hAodha, Mac Aodha; see McCoy and compare McHugh .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: Marion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.

historicEvents.title: A Treaty with Hawaii
historicEvents.description: In the Mid 1870s, The United States sought out the Kingdom of Hawaii to make a free trade agreement. The Treaty gave the Hawaiians access to the United States agricultural markets and it gave the United States a part of land which later became Pearl Harbor.

historicEvents.title: Yellow Fever Epidemic
historicEvents.description: When a man that had escaped a quarantined steamboat with yellow fever went to a restaurant he infected Kate Bionda the owner. This was the start of the yellow fever epidemic in Memphis, Tennessee. By the end of the epidemic 5,200 of the residence would die.

historicEvents.title: Plessy vs. Ferguson
historicEvents.description: A landmark decision of the U.S. Supreme Court upholding the constitutionality of racial segregation laws for public facilities if the segregated facilities were equal in quality. It's widely regarded as one of the worst decisions in U.S. Supreme Court history.

historicEvents.title: War with the Spanish
historicEvents.description: After the explosion of the USS Maine in the Havana Harbor in Cuba, the United States engaged the Spanish in war. The war was fought on two fronts, one in Cuba, which helped gain their independence, and in the Philippines, which helped the US gain another territory for a time.

en.lifeSummary: When General Richard Marion Hughes was born on 19 June 1834, in Hamilton, Tennessee, United States, his father, William Hughes, was 29 and his mother, Matilda Clingman, was 29. He married Mary Elizabeth Magby on 15 September 1851, in Clark, Arkansas, United States. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 6 daughters. He lived in Henderson Township, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States in 1900 and Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States in 1910. He died in March 1914, in Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States, at the age of 79.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando General Richard Marion Hughes nasceu em 19 de junho de 1834, em Hamilton, Tenessi, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, William Hughes, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Clingman, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Elizabeth Magby em 15 de setembro de 1851, em Clark, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele viveu em Henderson Township, Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1900 e em Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1910. Ele faleceu em março de 1914, em Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда General Richard Marion Hughes родился 19 июня 1834 г. в местности хамилтон, Теннесси, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени William Hughes было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Clingman, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Elizabeth Magby 15 сентября 1851 г. в местности кларк, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он жил в местности хендирсон тоуншип, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1900 году и в местности бисмаркк, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1910 году. Он умер марта 1914 г. в местности хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 79 лет.
